MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT RICHARD MOONEY, M.D. INC. DBA WOMEN'S HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's (RICHARD MOONEY, M.D. INC. dba WOMEN'S HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATES) motion to strike is granted and denied as follows:

The motion is denied as to the punitive damages allegations and prayer for exemplary damages. Plaintiff pleads sufficient ultimate facts and ratification to support the allegations. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC") pars. 26(o) and (r), 29, and 68.) The motion, however, is granted as to paragraph 144 and the claim for punitives on the twelfth cause of action, which sounds in negligence, as conceded.

The motion is granted as to the prayer for "all statutory damages and/or Penalties as described herein in the California Civil Code, the California Government Code, or the California Labor Code, or as otherwise provided by law." Plaintiff does not allege the Labor and Government Code claims against Mooney. As to cause of action four under the Civil Code, the requested relief is unavailable. The motion is also granted as to the prayer for attorney's fees. Plaintiff does not highlight a basis for that recovery based on the claims she asserts against Mooney.

The motion is granted as to the prayer for declaratory relief. Plaintiff does not allege a claim to support the relief. Declaratory relief is designed to provide prospective relief and the claim is not necessary or proper. (See Snyder v. California Ins. Guarantee Assn. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1207 ["Declaratory relief operates prospectively, and not merely for the redress of past wrongs." (cleaned up)]; CCP 1061 [court discretion to deny declaratory relief].)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)
